summary: chai-chain-dom fetches and executes remote JavaScript via detached child process
details: The package impersonates pino/chai-await-dom (README, badges, and decoy files under lib/ mirror pino's structure) while its actual behavior is a remote-code loader. index.js (the package main) spawns lib/caller.js as a detached `node` child with stdio ignored and unref()ed, so it survives the parent. lib/caller.js issues an HTTPS GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/BPB86, reads the `.model` field of the JSON response, and executes it via `new Function.constructor('require', src)(require)`, retrying up to 5 times. The remote payload is attacker-mutable (a public JSON paste endpoint) with no integrity check and is executed with full `require` access, giving the attacker arbitrary code execution on any machine that loads the package's exported middleware. The typosquat/impersonation of pino increases the likelihood of accidental installation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
